summary: 2026-06-29 19:09:47 PDT â€” Correctness review found two substantive issues: one internal contradiction in the dev-audit read-only tool instructions, and one README/system-map drift that the new linter does not catch. `bash tests/dev_audit_test.sh` and `bash tests/reviewer_test.sh` pass; the full configured gate could not complete in this read-only sandbox because `tests/guard_test.sh` needs to create temporary git repos.

findings.title: PHP read-only command contradicts the blanket AC7 rule
findings.claim: The PHP row tells Claude to run `php-cs-fixer fix --dry-run`, which is the normal non-writing form, but step 4 later forbids `fix`/`format` without `--check`. Since step 4 also says to invoke tools exactly as Table A specifies, PHP audits now have conflicting instructions for the read-only invariant.
findings.suggestion: Align the rule and table: either allow `fix`/`format` when paired with `--dry-run` as well as `--check`, or replace the PHP row with a supported check-mode command. Add a drift assertion for whichever wording is chosen.

findings.title: README still has stale pre-dev-audit system-map text
findings.claim: The README artifact trail still says `BACKLOG.md` contains only `BUG-` and `OPS-` items graduating to `reviews/<slug>.md`, omitting `AUDIT-` items and `reviews/audit-<YYYY-MM-DD>.md`. The native-workflow section also still says only `/frame`, `/review`, and `/close` stop on `docs/ai-protocol.md`, contradicting the new `/dev-audit` section and the skill's stand-down rule. `tests/dev_audit_test.sh` only greps the README for `/dev-audit`, so this stale AC8 doc drift passes the linter.
findings.suggestion: Update README's artifact trail and native-workflow sections to include `AUDIT-`, the audit report artifact, and `/dev-audit` stand-down behavior; strengthen the linter to anchor those README tokens, not just the existence of `/dev-audit`.

findings.title: Backlog OPS-9 still scopes frontmatter review to three skills
findings.claim: The live OPS-9 item still says it evaluates frontmatter for the three skills `frame`, `review`, and `close`. This branch adds `dev-audit` with the same `name` + `description` frontmatter convention, so the backlog now contains a stale token that excludes the new skill from that active evaluation item.
findings.suggestion: Reword OPS-9 to cover the workflow skills including `dev-audit`, or explicitly say the item intentionally excludes `/dev-audit`.
